meta.description: Curated internship and career experience programs for the LabLink Initiative CDP. All programs are paid unless noted. Data verified against official program websites.

programs.name: DOE Science Undergraduate Laboratory Internships (SULI)

eligibility.notes: Must be enrolled at an accredited college/university. Full-time enrollment not required.

compensation.stipend: weekly stipend (varies by lab and major)

locations: Thomas Jefferson National Accelerator Facility, VA
keyFacts: Flagship DOE undergraduate internship program
17+ national laboratories participate
Research on cutting-edge science and technology
Mentored by DOE lab scientists and engineers
Can apply to multiple labs simultaneously on Zintellect
programs.lliBridgeNote: LLI co-hosted a webinar with INL and PTK focused on SULI. Our free guide covers SULI application strategy.

programs.name: DOE Community College Internships (CCI)

eligibility.notes: Must be currently enrolled in an accredited 2-year community college or technical school.

compensation.stipend: weekly stipend (varies by lab and major)

locations: Thomas Jefferson National Accelerator Facility, VA
keyFacts: Designed specifically for community college students
One of the most undersubscribed DOE programs — strong acceptance rates
Technical and vocational backgrounds welcome alongside traditional STEM
Same national laboratory network as SULI
A direct LLI flagship program — we specialize in helping CC students access CCI
programs.lliBridgeNote: CCI is LLI's origin program. Sean and Michael both accessed national labs through community college pathways like this. CCI has only ~18-21 slots per lab per term — it is massively undersubscribed.

programs.name: AFRL Scholars Program

eligibility.notes: Must be able to obtain a security clearance. US citizenship required.

keyFacts: Air Force Research Laboratory — cutting-edge defense research
Path to federal career and security clearance
9 directorates across the country
Mentored by AFRL scientists and engineers
Sean Florez (LLI co-founder) interned at AFRL
programs.lliBridgeNote: Sean Florez completed an AFRL internship during his path from community college to Ph.D. AFRL is a core LLI partner program.

programs.name: NASA Internships

eligibility.notes: Most positions require US citizenship. GPA requirements vary by center and project.

locations: Goddard Space Flight Center, Greenbelt MD
Ames Research Center, Mountain View CA
Marshall Space Flight Center, Huntsville AL
keyFacts: 3 application cycles per year
Business, communications, and policy roles available alongside STEM
JPL positions may require additional clearance process
Strong alumni network across aerospace and defense industry
programs.lliBridgeNote: NASA internships span all disciplines — STEM and Business students both qualify. Many positions do not require specialized coursework beyond general science/engineering foundation.

programs.name: EPA Greater Research Opportunities (GRO) Fellowship

eligibility.notes: For junior-year undergrads; includes summer internship at an EPA facility.

deadlines.main: November (for following academic year)

keyFacts: Combines academic-year financial support with summer EPA internship
Managed by ORISE (same portal as DOE programs)
Strong pathway to environmental science federal careers
Especially relevant for biology, chemistry, and public health majors
programs.lliBridgeNote: EPA GRO is through the same ORISE/Zintellect platform as DOE SULI and CCI. If you're already applying to SULI, adding EPA GRO is a low-lift additional option.

programs.name: NIST Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowship (SURF)

eligibility.notes: Sophomore standing or above preferred.

keyFacts: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Measurement science and standards — unique research area
Less competitive than NASA/DOE programs — strong fit for students wanting federal lab exposure
Strong networking with federal career paths
programs.lliBridgeNote: NIST is often overlooked but is a flagship US research institution. NIST SURF is a high-quality alternative when SULI competition is steep.

programs.name: NSF Research Experiences for Undergraduates (REU)

eligibility.notes: Each REU site sets its own criteria. Many sites prioritize community college students and underrepresented groups.

compensation.housing: most sites provide housing or housing allowance

locations: 700+ sites across all 50 states — universities, colleges, and research centers
researchAreas: All STEM disciplines — biology, chemistry, physics, engineering, computer science, mathematics, materials science, social sciences, geosciences, and more
programs.applicationPlatform: NSF REU Site Search + individual site portals
keyFacts: 700+ sites annually across all STEM disciplines
Hosted at universities, not just federal labs
Many sites specifically recruit community college students
Strong launchpad for graduate school
Apply to multiple sites — requirements vary widely
programs.lliBridgeNote: NSF REUs are geographically distributed so students can find sites near home or at top research universities. Excellent for students not yet ready for federal lab competition.

programs.name: NIH Summer Internship Program in Biomedical Research (SIP)

eligibility.notes: Accepts a wide range of students from high school through postbac. Strong emphasis on biomedical and health sciences.

compensation.housing: not provided (Bethesda MD cost of living assistance varies)

locations: NIH Rocky Mountain Labs, Hamilton MT
keyFacts: National Institutes of Health — world's largest biomedical research institution
Highly competitive but accepts diverse academic backgrounds
Access to top scientists across 27 institutes and centers
Strong pathway to biomedical graduate programs and medical school
programs.lliBridgeNote: NIH SIP broadens LLI's footprint into biomedical careers — particularly valuable for biology, pre-med, and public health students.

programs.name: NOAA Ernest F. Hollings Undergraduate Scholarship

eligibility.notes: Must be sophomore at time of application. All STEM and social science disciplines considered.

compensation.stipend: $9,500 academic year scholarship × 2 years; $700/week summer internship

deadlines.main: January 31 (annual)

keyFacts: 2-year scholarship plus summer internship at NOAA
One of the most comprehensive federal science scholarships
Full mentorship and career development support
Highly prestigious — strong resume differentiator
programs.lliBridgeNote: Hollings combines scholarship + internship — exceptional value for a strong sophomore applicant. Worth the additional preparation investment.

programs.name: DOE Energy I-Corps (EIC) Technology Commercialization Program

eligibility.notes: For graduate students and postdocs working on energy technology commercialization.

deadlines.main: Rolling — 2-3 cohorts per year

keyFacts: Focused on technology commercialization, not just research
Graduate students pair with industry mentors
Strong pathway to DOE SBIR/STTR and startup ecosystem
Less known but high-value for entrepreneurially-minded grad students
programs.lliBridgeNote: Appropriate for grad students in LLI's network interested in clean energy entrepreneurship beyond just research careers.

programs.name: Idaho National Laboratory Internship Program

eligibility.notes: INL has additional paid positions beyond SULI/CCI. Background check required.

compensation.housing: not typically provided (Idaho Falls is affordable)

deadlines.main: Rolling — positions posted on INL careers page

keyFacts: Michael Sarmento (LLI co-founder) interned at INL
INL is the only US nuclear energy laboratory — unique specialty
INL has the most nuclear engineering internship slots of any DOE lab
Nuclear internships increasingly include cybersecurity and AI angles
INL co-hosted LLI's first webinar with PTK
programs.lliBridgeNote: INL is LLI's founding lab partner. Michael's path from community college to INL is the origin story. We have direct relationships with INL's internship office.

programs.name: Oak Ridge National Laboratory Internships

eligibility.notes: ORNL manages many SULI/CCI positions plus direct lab appointments.

compensation.stipend: SULI rates apply for ORISE positions; direct hires are competitive hourly

deadlines.main: Apply via Zintellect for SULI/CCI; ORNL careers portal for direct hires

keyFacts: Home to Frontier supercomputer — world's most powerful as of 2022
Operates the Spallation Neutron Source and High Flux Isotope Reactor
Strong in advanced manufacturing (MDF) and AI/ML research
Major employer in the Southeast with strong community college pipeline
programs.lliBridgeNote: ORNL's advanced manufacturing and AI research is increasingly accessible to engineering and CS students. Strong program for Southeast-based students.

programs.name: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory Student Programs

eligibility.notes: Many LLNL positions require Q clearance eligibility (US citizenship + background check). No prior clearance needed — LLNL sponsors.

compensation.stipend: SULI rates + LLNL direct hire competitive pay

deadlines.main: Rolling — LLNL careers + Zintellect for SULI

keyFacts: Michael Sarmento (LLI co-founder) interned at LLNL via NNSA
Home to the National Ignition Facility — achieved nuclear fusion ignition 2022
Strong path to national security careers and Q clearance
Bay Area location provides strong tech industry networking
Data science, AI, and computing roles growing rapidly
programs.lliBridgeNote: LLNL is Michael's lab connection. National security science and computing are growing areas — many non-physics roles available.

programs.name: Sandia National Laboratories Student Internships

eligibility.notes: Most positions require security clearance eligibility. Sandia has some of the most competitive SULI slots.

keyFacts: Sandia is NNSA's engineering laboratory — high-value national security research
Engineering roles dominate but CS, data science growing fast
New Mexico location offers lower cost of living than coastal labs
Strong co-op program for multi-term commitments
programs.lliBridgeNote: Sandia is a top-tier target for engineering, CS, and physical science students. The Albuquerque location is accessible for Southwest students.

programs.name: Pacific Northwest National Laboratory Internships

eligibility.notes: PNNL actively recruits Pacific Northwest community college students.

deadlines.main: Zintellect for SULI/CCI; PNNL careers for direct

keyFacts: Strong data analytics and grid modernization research
Actively recruits Tri-Cities area community college students
Environmental science and Pacific Northwest ecology research
Cybersecurity and national security research growing
programs.lliBridgeNote: PNNL is particularly accessible for Pacific Northwest and Tri-Cities area community college students.

programs.name: National Renewable Energy Laboratory Internships

eligibility.notes: NREL is the only DOE lab dedicated entirely to renewable energy — strong demand for clean energy-specific backgrounds.

compensation.stipend: SULI rates + NREL direct competitive rates

deadlines.main: Zintellect for SULI/CCI; NREL careers for direct

keyFacts: The only US national lab 100% focused on renewable energy
Golden, CO location — Denver metro, excellent quality of life
Direct pipeline into clean energy industry jobs
Strong internship-to-hire conversion rate
Business, policy, and finance roles available alongside engineering
programs.lliBridgeNote: NREL is the flagship clean energy lab. Business Analytics, Policy, and Finance students can find non-engineering roles there — not just STEM.

programs.name: Argonne National Laboratory Student Programs

eligibility.notes: Argonne has the largest computing and physics programs of any DOE lab.

deadlines.main: Zintellect for SULI/CCI; ANL careers for direct

keyFacts: Home to Aurora supercomputer — the first US exascale computer
Chicagoland location — affordable Midwest cost of living
Strong physics, computing, and advanced battery research
APS (Advanced Photon Source) — synchrotron used by research from worldwide
programs.lliBridgeNote: Argonne's computing and AI research is cutting-edge. CS and data science students have strong options here alongside traditional STEM.

programs.name: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory Internships

eligibility.notes: Berkeley Lab is unique — directly affiliated with UC Berkeley.

compensation.stipend: SULI rates + LBNL direct (Bay Area adjustment)

deadlines.main: Zintellect for SULI/CCI; LBNL careers for direct

keyFacts: On the UC Berkeley campus — unique lab-university integration
Nobel laureates are common here — the scientific environment is exceptional
Active Community College Internship program specifically for Bay Area CC students
Bay Area tech ecosystem creates unique networking for CS/data students
programs.lliBridgeNote: LBNL actively runs a Community College Internship alongside CCI. Bay Area CC students have a strong local pipeline here.

programs.name: DOE Scholars Program

eligibility.notes: Managed by ORISE. Placements at DOE Headquarters and field sites (not always national labs).

compensation.stipend: weekly stipend + housing allowance for many positions

locations: DOE field offices and sites nationwide

deadlines.main: Rolling — positions posted on Zintellect year-round

keyFacts: DOE Headquarters placements — policy, communications, operations roles
Good option for Business, Policy, Communications students who want federal exposure
Often less competitive than laboratory-specific programs
Direct pathway to understanding federal energy policy
programs.lliBridgeNote: DOE Scholars opens national lab and federal agency pathways to Business and Policy students — not just STEM. Underutilized and less competitive.

programs.name: ORAU Lab-to-Lab Student Internship

eligibility.notes: Managed by ORAU for DOE labs. Less known but high quality.

deadlines.main: February-March

keyFacts: Managed by ORAU (Oak Ridge Associated Universities)
Less publicized than SULI but comparable quality
Good backup option for students already pursuing SULI
programs.lliBridgeNote: ORAU manages multiple DOE student programs. Students should check the ORAU portal directly alongside Zintellect.

programs.name: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program (GRFP)

eligibility.notes: Undergrads can apply in senior year. One of the most prestigious fellowships in science and engineering.

deadlines.main: October (varies by discipline)

keyFacts: One of the most prestigious early-career science fellowships
Apply as a senior — 3 years of $37K stipend in grad school
The 'NSF fellowship' on a CV opens major doors in academic and government science
Research freedom — bring your own project to any grad program
programs.lliBridgeNote: GRFP is the graduate transition for high-achieving SULI/REU alumni. LLI can help students position their national lab experience in GRFP essays.

programs.name: HHMI Gilliam Fellowships for Advanced Study

eligibility.notes: For PhD students from groups underrepresented in biomedical sciences. Applied through advisor.

deadlines.main: Varies — institution-based nomination

keyFacts: Focused on diversity in biomedical science
Nominated through advisor — mentorship development is part of program
Strong network of HHMI alumni in academic and industry science
programs.lliBridgeNote: Relevant for LLI's life science students transitioning to PhD programs.

programs.name: Smithsonian Institution Internship Program

eligibility.notes: Wide range of fields — STEM, arts, education, communications, museum studies.

compensation.stipend: some paid positions available ($700/week for select programs)

keyFacts: Not all positions paid — apply strategically
Unique access to world-class science and cultural collections
Strong pathway for museum science, conservation, and policy careers
Humanities and social science students welcome alongside STEM
programs.lliBridgeNote: Smithsonian broadens LLI's portfolio to include humanities and social science students interested in federal science institutions.

programs.name: DOE Office of Science Graduate Student Research (SCGSR) Program

eligibility.notes: Must be enrolled in PhD program at accredited US institution. Thesis must be related to DOE Office of Science research priorities.

locations: All 17 DOE Office of Science National Laboratories

deadlines.main: May and November (two annual solicitations)

keyFacts: PhD students work at national labs while maintaining grad school enrollment
Research must relate to DOE Office of Science priorities — defined broadly
Strong pathway from PhD to national lab career
Advisor and lab scientist must collaborate — builds relationship for post-PhD hiring
programs.lliBridgeNote: SCGSR is the graduate-level bridge to national lab careers — the grad school equivalent of SULI.

programs.name: Microsoft Explore Internship

eligibility.notes: Specifically designed for early-career CS students. Strong emphasis on diversity.

keyFacts: Rotational program — 2 different teams in 12 weeks
Specifically for underclassmen (freshmen, sophomores)
Extremely high pay for early career
Strong conversion rate to full-time SWE roles
programs.lliBridgeNote: Industry internships at Microsoft caliber validate technical skills and fund graduate school or further lab work. LLI supports industry pathways alongside federal labs.

programs.name: Google STEP Internship

eligibility.notes: Focused on first- and second-year CS students from underrepresented groups.

locations: Mountain View CA, New York NY, other Google offices
keyFacts: Student Training in Engineering Program — DEI-focused
Pairs STEP interns with mentors for full summer
Direct pathway to Google SWE internship sophomore/junior year
Highly competitive but prioritizes underrepresented groups
programs.lliBridgeNote: STEP and similar industry programs complement LLI's national lab focus — CS students pursuing dual tracks (lab + industry) have very strong career outcomes.

programs.name: Lockheed Martin Engineering Internship

eligibility.notes: Defense contractor — US citizenship required. Many positions require clearance eligibility.

keyFacts: F-35, Orion spacecraft, Skunk Works — premier aerospace and defense work
Strong internship-to-hire conversion
Many locations require relocation — company supports
Industry + clearance combination opens many doors
programs.lliBridgeNote: Defense industry internships at Lockheed, Northrop, Raytheon complement DOE lab experience. For clearance-eligible students, this is a high-value combination.

programs.name: Tesla Energy Engineering Internship

eligibility.notes: Fast-paced environment. Strong in EE, ME, CS, Data.

deadlines.main: Rolling — posted year-round

keyFacts: Powerwall, Megapack, Solar Roof — direct clean energy product work
Fast-paced culture — expect high responsibility from day one
Battery technology experience directly overlaps with DOE lab research areas
Direct clean energy career — not government, but pure mission alignment
programs.lliBridgeNote: Industry clean energy companies like Tesla create direct application for the science students learn at national labs. A NREL internship + Tesla internship combination is a strong clean energy portfolio.

programs.name: NextEra Energy Engineering & Analytics Internship

eligibility.notes: World's largest producer of wind and solar energy.

locations: various US wind and solar sites
keyFacts: Operates Florida Power & Light + 100+ GW of wind and solar
Business Analytics and Finance roles available alongside engineering
One of the largest employers in clean energy outside of government labs
Strong graduate hiring pipeline
programs.lliBridgeNote: NextEra is a premier industry destination for Business Analytics and Engineering students in clean energy. Business students have strong options here.

programs.name: PTK Career Connect (Phi Theta Kappa)

eligibility.notes: Phi Theta Kappa honor society membership required (3.5 GPA, 12 college credits).

compensation.stipend: scholarships and awards available through PTK

deadlines.main: Rolling — join through your campus PTK chapter

keyFacts: 3.5M+ members — largest honor society for 2-year colleges
Michael Sarmento (LLI co-founder) was PTK Chapter President at SCC
PTK membership signals academic achievement on applications
LLI co-hosted October 2025 webinar with PTK and INL
PTK provides transfer scholarship access and career resources
programs.lliBridgeNote: PTK is LLI's primary community college partner. PTK members who qualify for SULI/CCI have a direct pipeline through LLI.

programs.name: Zintellect / ORISE Research Opportunities

eligibility.notes: Platform hosting SULI, CCI, EPA GRO, DOE Scholars, and 200+ other DOE/federal programs.

deadlines.main: Multiple deadlines throughout the year — SULI/CCI in January

keyFacts: One profile, access to 200+ DOE and federal research programs
SULI, CCI, EPA GRO, SCGSR, DOE Scholars all on one platform
Create your profile once, apply to multiple programs and labs
The #1 tool for DOE lab internship access
programs.lliBridgeNote: CRITICAL: Every LLI student should create a Zintellect profile. This is the single highest-leverage action for DOE lab internship access.

programs.name: Handshake — Career Platform

eligibility.notes: Student-facing platform. Many national labs and federal agencies post directly here.

deadlines.main: Varies by posting

keyFacts: INL, Sandia, Argonne, ORNL all post directly to Handshake
Used by 1,400+ community colleges and universities
Good for finding internships not on Zintellect
Free for students
programs.lliBridgeNote: Students should maintain both Zintellect and Handshake profiles. Many INL direct hires come through Handshake.

programs.name: HACU National Internship Program (HNIP)

eligibility.notes: Must be enrolled at a Hispanic-Serving Institution (HSI) OR be of Hispanic/Latino descent.

locations: Federal agencies nationwide (DOE, EPA, DOL, HHS, USDA, and more)
researchAreas: All federal agency focus areas — STEM, Business, Policy, Communications
keyFacts: Hispanic Association of Colleges and Universities program
Federal placements across many agencies — DOE, EPA, USDA, etc.
Strong for Business, Policy, and Communications students
ALPFA (LLI partner) serves similar Latino professional population
programs.lliBridgeNote: HACU HNIP is a key partner for LLI's ALPFA and Hispanic-serving community college relationships. Business and Policy students especially should apply.

programs.name: NSBE Career Fair & Internship Pipeline

eligibility.notes: NSBE membership recommended. Annual convention with massive career fair.

compensation.stipend: NSBE connects to paid internship employers

deadlines.main: Annual convention in March

keyFacts: National Society of Black Engineers
Annual convention career fair draws 200+ employers including DOE labs
NSBE membership signals commitment to professional development
Strong alumni network in engineering and tech
programs.lliBridgeNote: NSBE and LLI share target audiences. LLI should be present at NSBE convention to reach Black engineering students with national lab opportunities.

programs.name: Society of Women Engineers (SWE) Scholarships & Career Fair

eligibility.notes: Open to all engineering and technology students who identify as women.

locations: Annual WE conference + chapters at 1,000+ universities
keyFacts: Largest organization for women in engineering
Annual WE Conference career fair draws top employers including national labs
$1M+ in scholarships awarded annually
Strong professional network and mentoring programs
programs.lliBridgeNote: SWE and LLI should co-promote — SWE scholarships plus LLI internship placement is a strong value package for women engineering students.
